catalog.tagline: Small enough for the city. Quiet enough for the morning.

emotional_features.headline: 350 km. A full week without looking for a charger.
emotional_features.body: The EX30 offers up to 350 km of WLTP range. Enough to get through the week on a single charge, without thinking twice about it.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: Freedom from routine anxiety. The charger disappears from your mental checklist.

emotional_features.headline: The size of a thought. The space of a life.
emotional_features.body: At 4.23 m long, it slips through the city where others hesitate — without ever making you feel cramped inside.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The city stops being a problem. You move through it like you own it.

emotional_features.headline: Silence as a starting point.
emotional_features.body: Fully electric, zero engine noise. Your mornings begin in a quiet you didn't expect to value this much.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The commute becomes the first breath of the day, not an ordeal.

emotional_features.headline: Harman Kardon in 4.23 metres. Sound that doesn't apologise for the size.
emotional_features.body: The optional Harman Kardon system fills the EX30's cabin with precision audio. Small car, no compromise on what you listen to on the way in.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: Your playlist, your podcast, your morning ritual — in the best possible sound.

emotional_features.headline: Google built-in. The city answers before you ask.
emotional_features.body: Google Maps, Google Assistant and the Play Store run natively. No cable, no mirroring — just the information you need at the moment you need it.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The car thinks ahead so you don't have to.

emotional_features.headline: The full Volvo safety suite. Nothing removed because of the price.
emotional_features.body: City Safety with pedestrian, cyclist and large animal detection. Run-off road protection. ISOFIX as standard. The EX30 is the smallest Volvo — not the most exposed.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You didn't compromise on safety to get the price you needed.

emotional_features.headline: Cloud Blue. Moss Yellow. Pine Green. A colour is not a detail.
emotional_features.body: The EX30 comes in colours that stop people in the street. A Volvo that is unmistakably Volvo — and unmistakably yours.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You chose something that reflects who you are, not what's safe.

config_options.label: EX30 Core — Single Motor
config_options.description: 272 km WLTP. The essentials done exceptionally well. Harman Kardon optional.

config_options.label: EX30 Extended Range — Single Motor
config_options.description: Up to 480 km WLTP. The range that removes every remaining doubt about going electric.

config_options.label: EX30 Twin Motor Performance
config_options.description: 315 kW. 0–100 in 3.6 s. For the mornings when quiet isn't quite enough.

tensions.label: You want electric, but you're not sure about the range.
tensions.resolution: The EX30 Extended Range covers 480 km WLTP — that's Brussels to Paris and back on a single charge. Most owners never charge more than twice a week.

tensions.label: You wonder if a small car means giving something up.
tensions.resolution: The EX30 is shorter than a Golf but taller inside. The floating centre console and panoramic roof make it feel larger than it measures.

catalog.tagline: The everyday companion that asks for nothing back.

emotional_features.headline: 600 km. Brussels to the south of France without a second thought.
emotional_features.body: The EX40 Extended Range reaches up to 600 km WLTP. Daily drives become routine. Weekends become something else entirely.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The weekend trip stops being a calculation. You just go.

emotional_features.headline: Pilot Assist — for the motorway kilometres when your mind needs to wander.
emotional_features.body: Pilot Assist holds your lane and your following distance on the motorway. You stay in control, but you're less alone in the task.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The drive becomes thinking time. The motorway gives something back.

emotional_features.headline: Harman Kardon. For the morning podcast, the evening playlist.
emotional_features.body: Optional Harman Kardon audio with 13 speakers tuned for the EX40 cabin. The commute becomes the best 40 minutes of your day.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: Sound that turns a routine drive into something you look forward to.

emotional_features.headline: It sees the pedestrian before you do. Day or night.
emotional_features.body: The EX40's City Safety system detects vehicles, pedestrians, cyclists and large animals around the clock. It warns first, then autobrakes if you don't react.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You drive with children in the back. The car never stops watching.

emotional_features.headline: ISOFIX. Power child lock. The details that don't make the brochure but matter every day.
emotional_features.body: ISOFIX anchorages for child seats are standard. Rear doors and windows lock from the driver's seat. A family SUV, thought through properly.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You stop worrying about the back seat. It's handled.

emotional_features.headline: Thor's Hammer headlights — they follow the road into the bend.
emotional_features.body: The EX40's LED headlights turn with the steering wheel, illuminating corners as you navigate them. Optional Pixel Technology for crisp, eye-friendly light on dark winter mornings.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: November at 7am stops being something to endure.

emotional_features.headline: 419 litres. The pushchair, the weekend bag, and something for the dog.
emotional_features.body: The EX40 boot swallows a family's week without negotiation. The flat floor and wide opening mean loading is never a second thought.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You stop playing Tetris with the boot before every trip.

emotional_features.headline: Twin Motor AWD. Because Belgium has winters too.
emotional_features.body: The EX40 Twin Motor sends torque to all four wheels instantly. Frost, wet leaves, the motorway in January — it reads the road before you feel it change.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: Winter mornings become just mornings again.

config_options.label: EX40 Single Motor — Standard Range
config_options.description: Up to 400 km WLTP. The daily driver that never asks to be noticed.

config_options.label: EX40 Extended Range + Harman Kardon + Pilot Assist
config_options.description: 600 km WLTP. The configuration for people who think in weekends, not just commutes.

config_options.label: EX40 Twin Motor AWD + ISOFIX + Pilot Assist
config_options.description: AWD traction, full family safety suite. The configuration that removes every parental doubt.

tensions.label: You want to go fully electric but the long trips worry you.
tensions.resolution: The EX40 Extended Range covers 600 km WLTP. With fast charging at 150 kW, a 10–80% charge takes under 30 minutes on a motorway stop.

tensions.label: You need a family car but don't want to feel like you gave up on yourself.
tensions.resolution: The EX40 drives like a Volvo, not a people-carrier. Harman Kardon, Pilot Assist, and a genuinely beautiful interior — it's a car you'd have chosen anyway.

tensions.label: The EX90 is the dream but the EX40 is the reality.
tensions.resolution: The EX40 Extended Range gives you 600 km, AWD, Pilot Assist and Harman Kardon. The gap to the EX90 is real — but what you gain at this price is complete.

catalog.tagline: Built for the people you protect.

emotional_features.headline: LiDAR sees 250 metres ahead. You're watching your daughter.
emotional_features.body: The EX90 is the first Volvo with Luminar LiDAR as standard. It maps the environment in 3D, in real time — while you're having the conversation that matters.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The car is present so you can be present. Two attentions, perfectly divided.

emotional_features.headline: It knows when you're not quite there. Before you do.
emotional_features.body: The Driver Understanding System detects fatigue and distraction through cabin sensors. If you don't respond to its alerts, it slows the car and calls for help.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The long drive home after the late night. Someone is watching out for you.

emotional_features.headline: The suspension adapts 500 times per second. The Ardennes simply leave the conversation.
emotional_features.body: The EX90's active chassis continuously adjusts damper stiffness. Cobblestones, forest roads, the motorway at 130 — each surface is met with the same calm.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The journey stops being something to get through. It becomes time you have.

emotional_features.headline: 7 seats. Including two that only fill up when everyone is there.
emotional_features.body: The third row comfortably fits adults. For the school run where someone's friend needs a lift, or the holiday where nobody gets left behind.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The car grows with the moment. Nobody sits out.

emotional_features.headline: Bowers & Wilkins. 25 speakers that know the difference between music and silence.
emotional_features.body: The EX90's Bowers & Wilkins system was built to reveal what was recorded, not to impress. Seven amplifiers. Diamond tweeter. Audio calibrated for the specific acoustics of this cabin.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The drive to the coast. The album that belongs to that road.

emotional_features.headline: 600 km. The only electric SUV that doesn't ask you to plan around it.
emotional_features.body: The EX90 Twin Motor Extended Range covers up to 600 km WLTP. Family logistics, the long weekend, the unexpected detour — none of it requires a calculation.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You stopped thinking about charging. That's the real luxury.

emotional_features.headline: Vehicle-to-grid. Your car powers the house on Sunday morning.
emotional_features.body: The EX90 can feed energy back to your home. On a weekend morning, the house runs on the car while it sits in the driveway. It is a different relationship with electricity.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The car becomes part of how the home works. Not just how you move.

config_options.label: EX90 Twin Motor — Standard Range
config_options.description: 517 km WLTP. 7 seats, LiDAR, Bowers & Wilkins standard. The entry point to the flagship.

config_options.label: EX90 Twin Motor Extended Range + Bowers & Wilkins + Air Suspension
config_options.description: 600 km WLTP. The configuration that removes every remaining compromise.

tensions.label: The price is serious. You need to justify it.
tensions.resolution: The EX90 replaces two anxieties at once: range anxiety and the question of whether your family is as safe as possible on the road. LiDAR, Driver Understanding, 7 seats, 600 km — at this level, the car stops being a cost and becomes a decision you made once.

tensions.label: Seven seats and fully electric — you wonder if the range holds up.
tensions.resolution: 600 km WLTP in the Extended Range. With 250 kW DC fast charging, you add 150 km in 10 minutes. The only family electric SUV that doesn't require a new mental model.

catalog.tagline: The Volvo that knows your weekends by heart.

emotional_features.headline: Electric for 70 km. Petrol for everything else.
emotional_features.body: The XC60 Recharge covers up to 70 km on pure electric — enough for most weeks without touching the combustion engine. On the weekend, the range extends to wherever you decide to go.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You don't have to choose between conviction and convenience. The XC60 holds both.

emotional_features.headline: AWD torque vectoring — the road commits to you before you commit to it.
emotional_features.body: The XC60 AWD distributes torque between all four wheels in real time. The Ardennes in November, the forest track at dawn — it always finds grip.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You stop adjusting your plans to the weather. The car adjusts instead.

emotional_features.headline: Pilot Assist — because the motorway deserves more than your full attention.
emotional_features.body: On the motorway, Pilot Assist holds your lane and your distance. You arrive at the chalet less tired than you left Brussels.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The drive to the mountains becomes part of the holiday, not the prelude to it.

emotional_features.headline: CleanZone. The cabin air is filtered before anyone notices.
emotional_features.body: The XC60's CleanZone system filters particulates and allergens from the cabin air. It starts working before you get in.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The morning commute stops being something that arrives at your desk with you.

emotional_features.headline: Google built-in. The route is ready before you've buckled in.
emotional_features.body: Google Maps runs natively in the XC60 — real-time traffic, parking, charging stops pre-loaded. The car knows where you're going before you've said it.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The friction of the departure disappears. You just leave.

emotional_features.headline: Optional Bower & Wilkins. For the drive that deserves a score.
emotional_features.body: The XC60's optional Bowers & Wilkins system brings 15 speakers tuned to the cabin. For the route through the Hautes-Fagnes with the windows down and nothing urgent.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The road becomes the destination. The music is the reason.

emotional_features.headline: 360° Park Assist Camera. Tight spots in the city, handled.
emotional_features.body: The XC60's 360° camera shows every angle simultaneously. The tight space on the Rue Neuve, the underground parking in the dark — it's no longer an event.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: One of the small daily irritations simply stops existing.

config_options.label: XC60 B5 Mild Hybrid AWD
config_options.description: 286 hp, AWD, mild-hybrid. The driver's XC60 — responsive, planted, no plug required.

config_options.label: XC60 Recharge PHEV AWD + Pilot Assist + CleanZone
config_options.description: 70 km electric. AWD. The configuration for the person who wants to do the right thing without giving anything up.

config_options.label: XC60 Recharge PHEV AWD + Bowers & Wilkins + Pilot Assist
config_options.description: The weekend-escape configuration. Electric in the city, AWD in the Ardennes, Bowers & Wilkins for everything in between.

tensions.label: You want to go off the beaten path but not sacrifice refinement.
tensions.resolution: The XC60 AWD was developed on Scandinavian roads in winter. It handles what Belgium and the Ardennes ask of it — without becoming a different car.

tensions.label: You want to reduce your footprint but you're not ready for full electric.
tensions.resolution: The XC60 Recharge covers most of your week on pure electric and switches seamlessly to hybrid when you need more. It's the transition that doesn't feel like one.

catalog.tagline: Some journeys ask for room. And quiet.

emotional_features.headline: Bowers & Wilkins. 19 speakers that understand when silence is the right answer.
emotional_features.body: The XC90's Bowers & Wilkins system was calibrated for silence as much as sound. Not to mask road noise — to occupy exactly the right amount of space in the cabin.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The long drive becomes the concert. The family asleep in the back, the road ahead, the music that belongs to that hour.

emotional_features.headline: Air suspension — the Ardennes, the motorway, the car park. One ride.
emotional_features.body: The optional air suspension adjusts ride height and damping automatically. What you feel inside is always calm. What you see through the window changes — the car does not.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You stop noticing the road. You notice everything else.

emotional_features.headline: Electric for 50 km. Then as far as the family needs.
emotional_features.body: The XC90 Recharge runs up to 50 km on pure electric — the school run, the errands, the weekly commute. The combustion engine is there for the weekend, not the routine.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The routine runs on electricity. The weekends run on possibility.

emotional_features.headline: The boot that never says no.
emotional_features.body: The XC90 boot opens hands-free with a kick gesture. Wide, flat, no loading lip. The double pram, the ski bags, the dog, the entire long weekend — it all goes in. Without the conversation.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You stop mentally allocating the boot before you've packed. It's already solved.

emotional_features.headline: A third row that treats adults as adults.
emotional_features.body: The XC90's third row offers genuine headroom and legroom for adults. Not for children who have no choice — for the grandparents, the colleagues, the friends who came along.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: Nobody has to draw the short straw. Everyone arrives the same.

emotional_features.headline: Pilot Assist on the long road. Because the drive there is part of the holiday.
emotional_features.body: On the motorway, the XC90 holds your lane and distance while you stay attentive. Brussels to the Alps — the car takes some of the distance so you can give more of yourself to the destination.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You arrive at the chalet having travelled, not having fought.

emotional_features.headline: Inscription. The interior that justifies the silence outside.
emotional_features.body: Nappa leather, open-pore driftwood, crystal gear selector. The XC90 Inscription trim turns the cabin into a reason to linger — not just to arrive.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You sit in it and understand immediately. This is what it was supposed to feel like.

config_options.label: XC90 Recharge T8 AWD — Core
config_options.description: 50 km electric, 7 seats, AWD. The complete family car, before the options.

config_options.label: XC90 Recharge Inscription + Air Suspension + Bowers & Wilkins
config_options.description: The flagship as it was meant to be experienced. Nappa leather, air ride, 19 speakers.

tensions.label: You want the space and presence of an XC90 but wonder if it's excessive.
tensions.resolution: The XC90 isn't excessive for a family that uses all seven seats. It's the car that grows with the moment — the extra row only exists when you need it, and disappears when you don't.

tensions.label: You want to move toward electric but seven seats and long trips complicate it.
tensions.resolution: The XC90 Recharge gives you 50 km of daily electric driving and unlimited hybrid range for the weekends. It's the bridge that doesn't feel like a compromise.

catalog.tagline: For the lives that don't end at the road.

emotional_features.headline: 529 litres — for the weekends that don't fit in a suitcase.
emotional_features.body: The V60 boot offers 529 litres, expandable to 1,441 with the rear seats folded. The bikes, the dog, the ski gear, the camping kit — everything fits. Without negotiation.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You stop editing what you take. You take everything you need.

emotional_features.headline: Pilot Assist — the North Sea is closer than it looks.
emotional_features.body: On the motorway, the V60 holds your lane and distance. The drive to the coast, the long return — less effort, same presence. The journey stops being something to endure.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The long drive becomes time, not distance.

emotional_features.headline: AWD — because the weather doesn't warn you.
emotional_features.body: The V60 AWD distributes power between axles intelligently. Wet November roads, frosted morning tarmac, the forest track in October — it anticipates what you don't have time to calculate.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You leave in any weather. The question stops being whether, and becomes where.

emotional_features.headline: Recharge PHEV. Electric for the week. Free for the weekend.
emotional_features.body: The V60 Recharge covers up to 90 km on pure electric. The commute runs silently. On Friday evening, the full hybrid range takes over.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: Monday to Friday on electricity. The weekend on possibility.

emotional_features.headline: The Swedish wagon — and proud of it.
emotional_features.body: The V60 doesn't apologise for what it is. Clean lines, a low roofline, a stance that doesn't shout. In a world of SUVs, it is the considered choice.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: You chose it because it doesn't need to prove anything. Neither do you.

emotional_features.headline: Harman Kardon. The road has a soundtrack now.
emotional_features.body: Optional Harman Kardon with 14 speakers tuned for the V60 cabin. For the coastal drive, the early morning motorway, the long way home.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The route you've driven a hundred times becomes worth taking again.

emotional_features.headline: Cross Country raised suspension — for the road that isn't on the map.
emotional_features.body: The V60 Cross Country sits 75 mm higher, with underbody protection and AWD as standard. It goes where most wagons would turn around.
emotional_features.emotional_trigger: The detour becomes the reason you came.

config_options.label: V60 B4 Mild Hybrid — Core
config_options.description: 197 hp, mild-hybrid efficiency, Pilot Assist standard. The everyday V60.

config_options.label: V60 Recharge PHEV AWD + Harman Kardon
config_options.description: Up to 90 km electric, AWD, Harman Kardon. The full week on electricity, the full weekend on sound.

config_options.label: V60 Cross Country B5 AWD
config_options.description: +75 mm ground clearance, AWD, underbody protection. The wagon that doesn't need a road.

tensions.label: You need the boot space but don't want to drive something that feels like a van.
tensions.resolution: The V60 is 4.76 m long and handles like a car, not a box. The boot is the largest in its class. The two things coexist — this is what Volvo wagons have always been for.

tensions.label: It needs to work for the family but this is still your car.
tensions.resolution: The V60 was never designed as a concession. It's the car people choose when they've thought about it properly. Practical enough for everything, distinctive enough to mean something.

archive.title: OV4
archive.one_liner: The first Volvo. Hand-built for Swedish roads.
archive.story: On April 14, 1927, the first Volvo rolled out of Gothenburg. Engineer Gustaf Larson and economist Assar Gabrielsson agreed on one principle: cars are driven by people. The greatest safety value must therefore always be people themselves.
archive.illustration_hint: Black and white period photography of the OV4 in front of a wooden Gothenburg workshop, soft grain

archive.title: Laminated Safety Glass
archive.one_liner: The first windshield that refused to shatter.
archive.story: While the world was rebuilding, Volvo introduced laminated safety glass as standard on the PV444. A small decision that quietly saved countless faces.
archive.illustration_hint: Close-up of a windshield, muted post-war palette, soft daylight

archive.title: The Three-Point Seatbelt
archive.one_liner: An invention Volvo gave to the world.
archive.story: Engineer Nils Bohlin designed the three-point seatbelt. Volvo made the patent open. More than a million lives have been saved since. Some inventions are too important to belong to one company.
archive.illustration_hint: Nils Bohlin portrait next to a clean schematic of the three-point belt, Scandinavian minimalism

archive.title: The Environmental Declaration
archive.story: At the UN Conference on the Human Environment, Volvo became the first car maker to publicly commit to environmental responsibility. Long before it was marketable.
archive.illustration_hint: Vintage Volvo prototype with a Nordic landscape, soft documentary aesthetic

archive.title: Side Impact Protection
archive.one_liner: Protecting what no one had thought to protect.
archive.story: Volvo introduced the first Side Impact Protection System, building safety into the geometry of the car itself. The world followed.
archive.illustration_hint: Cross-section diagram of a 1991 Volvo 850, technical and elegant

archive.title: 180 km/h
archive.one_liner: We chose to slow down. On purpose.
archive.story: In 2019 Volvo capped every car at 180 km/h. A reminder that what a car can do, and what it should do, are not the same question.
archive.illustration_hint: A speedometer needle resting calmly below the redline, high contrast minimalism
